blockers.title: Partner admin writes bypass the required propose-confirm gate
blockers.evidence: Spec G16 requires partner create/edit, deliverable status updates and seal actions to be "all propose-confirm + audited" (specs/panel-experience.spec.md:125). The UI calls upsertPartner directly from onSave with the full partner record, including tier, status, contact, MOU, deliverables and show_publicly (src/admin/views/PartnerDetail.tsx:136-156), and the primary button invokes onSave directly (src/admin/views/PartnerDetail.tsx:417-429). New, relabelled or removed deliverables are only staged in form state and then written through that same direct save path (src/admin/views/PartnerDetail.tsx:510-512, src/admin/views/PartnerDetail.tsx:573-605). This violates the vault admin guardrail "Confirmation before every change" (/Users/ismac/Documents/Projects/WAI/02 Platform/02 Admin Approach - Agent-Operated.md:32-38).
blockers.required_fix: Put partner create/edit saves, deliverable add/remove/relabel/status changes and logo/public readiness writes behind the same propose-confirm pattern used for seal and saved-row status changes. Keep the backend audit rows, but do not let these writes commit from a single-click save.

blockers.title: Member-facing event meeting links are not server-side URL validated
blockers.evidence: upsertEvent accepts meeting_link as an optional string and stores meeting_link: args.meeting_link without a scheme or length allowlist (convex/admin/events.ts:187-208, convex/admin/events.ts:254-273). Registered members later receive that value as an href in the portal (src/portal/views/EventDetailView.tsx:286-292). The same backend validates recording and materials URLs as https before storage (convex/admin/events.ts:508-515), so the live meeting link path has a boundary-validation gap.
blockers.required_fix: Validate meeting_link in convex/admin/events.ts before storage, at minimum requiring a well-formed https URL with a sane max length, or an explicit allowlist of permitted meeting URL schemes. Add a regression test that calls the mutation directly, not only through the form.

non_blocking_notes: npx tsc --noEmit passed. npm test, npm run check and npm run build could not complete in this read-only sandbox because Vitest/Astro attempted to write generated cache files and hit EPERM.
git diff --check reports one formatting issue: src/portal/Dashboard.tsx has a new blank line at EOF.
convex/lib/standing.ts uses two .collect().then(...) chains, which violates the repo preference for async/await only, but it is not a ship blocker from this review.
No em dash/en dash matches, obvious secrets, raw HTML/eval sinks, or broad admin authorization omissions were found in the reviewed diff.

spec_compliance.evidence: Lane gating, RSVP, waitlist and check-in paths are implemented, but the member-facing meeting_link field is stored without server-side URL validation and rendered as an href for registered members.

Server paths enforce active lifecycle, profile completeness, lane eligibility, women_only ally exclusion, one application per member/opportunity, deadline refusal, auto-close and result notifications.

The automatic profile-complete plus qualifying-action promotion to active_member is implemented with standingHistory, audit and notification, and minors/restricted lanes do not receive directory promises.
Directory queries enforce active lifecycle, active_member+ standing, opted-in listing, standard/ally lanes only, and directory-tier fields only.
Own-row notification queries and writes are present for RSVP, waitlist promotion, event changes, applications, certificates and standing changes.
Admin member list/detail, audited contact reveal, legal lifecycle transitions, certificate revoke/reissue and super-admin gating are implemented without bulk export actions.
Backend audit exists, but create/edit and several deliverable writes bypass the required propose-confirm interaction.
Overview and reports use aggregate counts and task queues, with no individual export surface found.
No em dash/en dash matches were found in added member-facing strings; the workspace shells and modal vocabulary are present in the diff.
tsc passed, but the rest of the proof could not be completed in the read-only sandbox due EPERM writes from Vitest/Astro; git diff --check also reports a blank EOF issue.

summary: DO-NOT-SHIP. The slice misses an explicit partner admin propose-confirm acceptance criterion and has an unvalidated member-facing event meeting-link path.
